rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert annex content only within the owning component or implementation scope.
Keep declarations and references grounded in identifiers that are valid in the selected owner scope.
Maintain separation between type-level contracts and implementation-level content; do not move implementation bindings into type scope or vice versa.
generic_rules: Respect owner boundaries: preserve subcomponents, connections, features, and properties unchanged except for the intended annex insertion.
Use only symbols visible from the chosen owner scope; avoid introducing references to nested elements that are not valid there.
Apply minimal edits so the architecture and non-target model content remain intact.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level content belongs in implementation scope and should appear after relevant structural declarations when present.
anti_patterns: Do not alter architecture elements or property associations when performing annex insertion.
Do not carry over references to AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were in expression scope.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement semantics for specific conditions or outputs
Clause selection or contract content generation
Validator-driven repair actions without active diagnostics
